content.text:
Prove that for any norm on $\R^n$, the function $f(\mathbf{x}) = 
\|\mathbf{x}\|$ has condition number 1 at every $\mathbf{x} \neq 0$.

Let $f$ and $g$ be functions with condition numbers $\kappa_f$ 
and $\kappa_g$ at appropriate points. Prove that the condition number of 
the product $f(x) \cdot g(x)$ is at most $\kappa_f + \kappa_g$.

Show that if $f(x) = \sum_{k=0}^{n} a_k x^k$ is a polynomial 
and $|a_n x^n| \gg \sum_{k=0}^{n-1} |a_k x^k|$, then the condition number 
of $f$ at $x$ is approximately $n$.

Prove that round-to-nearest with ties-to-even is unbiased: 
if $x$ is uniformly distributed in an interval containing many floating-point 
numbers, then $\E[\fl(x) - x] = 0$.
